title: Finance officers
description: Organize activities to raise funds or otherwise solicit and gather monetary donations or other gifts for an organization. May design and produce promotional materials. May also raise awareness of the organization's work, goals, and financial needs. Locate and notify customers of delinquent accounts by mail, telephone, or personal visit to solicit payment. Duties include receiving payment and posting amount to customer's account, preparing statements to credit department if customer fails to respond, initiating repossession proceedings or service disconnection, and keeping records of collection and status of accounts. All financial clerks not listed separately. (Based on 8 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Receive payments and post amounts paid to customer accounts; answer customer questions regarding problems with their accounts; investigate and correct errors upon customers' request, according to customer and bank records; sort and file correspondence and perform miscellaneous clerical duties, such as answering correspondence and writing reports; keep records of customers' charges and payments.

Monitor budgets, expense reports, or other financial data for fundraising organizations; secure commitments of participation or donation from individuals or corporate donors; monitor progress of fundraising drives; explain the tax advantages of contributions to potential donors; advise customers of necessary actions and strategies for debt repayment; review customer accounts to determine whether payments are made on time; verify and examine information and accuracy of loan applications and closing documents; prepare and type loan applications, closing documents, legal documents, letters, forms, government notices, and checks, using computers.

Attend community events, meetings, or conferences to promote organizational goals or solicit donations or sponsorships; contact corporate representatives, government officials, or community leaders to increase awareness of organizational causes, activities, or needs; develop fundraising activity plans that maximize participation or contributions and minimize costs; develop strategies to encourage new or increased contributions; arrange for debt repayment or establish repayment schedules, based on customers' financial situations.

Create or update donor databases; develop corporate fundraising programs, such as employer gift-matching; develop or implement fundraising activities, such as annual giving campaigns or direct mail programs; compile or develop materials to submit to granting or other funding organizations; establish fundraising or participation goals for special events or specified time periods.

Design or produce materials such as posters, websites, or newsletters to promote, market, or advertise fundraising events; write speeches, press releases, or other promotional materials to increase awareness of the causes, missions, or goals of organizations seeking funds; write and send letters of thanks to donors; conduct research to identify the goals, net worth, charitable donation history, or other data related to potential donors, potential investors, or general donor markets; document security transactions, such as purchases, sales, conversions, redemptions, or payments, using computers, accounting ledgers, or certificate records.

Direct or supervise fundraising staff, including volunteer staff members; secure speakers for charitable events, community meetings, or conferences to increase awareness of charitable, nonprofit, or political causes; plan and direct special events for fundraising, such as silent auctions, dances, golf events, or walks; recruit sponsors, participants, or volunteers for fundraising events; direct or coordinate web-based fundraising activities, such as online auctions or donation websites; contact customers by mail, telephone, or in person concerning acceptance or rejection of applications; interview loan applicants to obtain personal and financial data and assist in completing applications.

Solicit cash or in-kind donations or sponsorships from individual, business, or government donors; trace delinquent customers to new addresses by inquiring at post offices, telephone companies, credit bureaus, or through the questioning of neighbors; locate and notify customers of delinquent accounts by mail, telephone, or personal visits to solicit payment; locate and monitor overdue accounts, using computers and a variety of automated systems; perform clerical tasks, such as answering phones or distributing mail; issue receipts and collect customer deposits and fees, using computers.
Contact credit bureaus, employers, and other sources to check applicants' credit and personal references; identify and build relationships with potential donors.

Compile information about new accounts, enter account information into computers, and file related forms or other documents; prepare forms, such as receipts, withdrawal orders, transmittal papers, or transfer confirmations, based on transaction requests from stockholders; issue initial and replacement safe-deposit keys to customers, and admit customers to vaults; collect initial premiums and issue receipts.

Answer telephones to direct calls or provide information.
Prepare financial documents, reports, or budgets.
Maintain data in information systems or databases.
Communicate organizational information to customers or other stakeholders.
Coordinate logistics or other business operations.
Search files, databases or reference materials to obtain needed information.
Determine the value of goods or services.
Discuss goods or services information with customers or patrons.
Confer with coworkers to coordinate work activities.
Correspond with customers to answer questions or resolve complaints.
Distribute materials to employees or customers.
